title: Semiclassical Geometric Tensor in Multiparameter Quantum Information
summary: The discrepancy between quantum distinguishability in Hilbert space and classical distinguishability in probability space is expressed by the gap between the quantum and classical Fisher information matrices (QFIM and CFIM, respectively). This intrinsic quantum obstruction is generally not saturable…

title: Observation of In-Ice Askaryan Radiation from High-Energy Cosmic Rays
summary: A South Pole neutrino experiment has measured radio waves induced by cosmic rays—thus demonstrating that its detection method works.

title: Cosmological Constant from Quantum Gravitational $θ$ Vacua and the Gravitational Hall Effect
summary: We provide a new perspective on the cosmological constant by exploring the background-independent Wheeler-DeWitt quantization of general relativity. The Chern-Simons-Kodama state of quantum gravity, a generalization of the Hartle-Hawking and Vilenkin states, has a striking structural similarity to t…

title: Universal Time Evolution of Holographic and Quantum Complexity
summary: Holographic complexity, as the bulk dual of quantum complexity, encodes the geometric structure of black hole interiors. Motivated by the complexity $=$ anything proposal, we introduce the spectral representation for generating functions associated with codimension-one and codimension-zero holograph…

title: Accessing Nucleon Transversity with One-Point Energy Correlators
summary: We propose a novel probe of the nucleon’s transversity distribution ${h}_{1}^{q}$ using the one-point energy correlator (OPEC), an infrared-and-collinear safe jet substructure observable. We demonstrate that in transversely polarized ${p}^{↑}p$ collisions, the OPEC exhibits a single-spin asymmetry (…

title: Precise Measurement of the $\mathrm{Λ}$-Binding-Energy Difference between $_{\mathrm{Λ}}^{3}\mathrm{H}$ and $_{\mathrm{Λ}}^{4}\mathrm{H}$ via Decay-Pion Spectroscopy at MAMI
summary: We performed high-precision decay-pion spectroscopy of light $\mathrm{Λ}$ hypernuclei at the Mainz Microtron (MAMI) using the A1 spectrometer facility. By measuring the monochromatic ${π}^{−}$ momentum from the two-body weak decay $_{\mathrm{Λ}}^{3}\mathrm{H}→^{3}\mathrm{He}+{π}^{−}$ and referencing…

title: Collapse of Nuclear Collectivity along the $N=Z$ Line
summary: The lifetime of the ${J}^{π}={2}^{+}$ state in the self-conjugate $^{88}\mathrm{Ru}$ nucleus has been determined in an experiment performed using rare-isotope beams provided by the new Facility for Rare Isotope Beams. This is the heaviest $N=Z$ nucleus for which such a measurement has been achieved.…

title: Host-Atom-Driven Transformation of a Honeycomb Oxide into a Dodecagonal Quasicrystal
summary: Dodecagonal oxide quasicrystals (OQCs) have so far been limited to a few elemental systems, with no general formation mechanism established. Here, we demonstrate a versatile approach to OQC formation via a host-atom-induced transformation of a metal-oxide honeycomb network. Adsorption of Ba, Sr, or …

title: Algebraic States in Continuum in $d>1$ Dimensional Non-Hermitian Systems
summary: We report the existence of algebraically localized eigenstates embedded within the continuum spectrum of 2D non-Hermitian systems with a single impurity. These modes, which we term algebraic states in continuum (AICs), decay algebraically as $1/|r|$ from the impurity site, and their energies lie wit…

title: Selective Ultrafast Optical Excitation of High-Wave-Number Dynamics in a Magnetophotonic Nanostructure
summary: Both the minimal size of optically switched magnetic bits and the wavelengths of optically excited spin waves in optomagnonic devices are fundamentally diffraction limited. Here, we propose and experimentally demonstrate a novel method to overcome these limitations by using a specially designed magn…

title: Giant Splitting and Universal Behavior of Fabry-Pérot Bound States in the Continuum in Waveguides
summary: It is always expected that tunneling coupling splits the energy of the eigenstates in proportion to its magnitude. This is also true for bound states in the continuum (BICs) in the waveguide strongly coupled to a Fabry-Pérot resonator. However, in the case of weak coupling, giant enhancement of the …

title: Entropy of Strongly Correlated Electrons in a Partially Filled Landau Level
summary: Entropy measurements in partially filled Landau levels reveal the successive freezing of spin and motional degrees of freedom, reaching sensitivities that constrain the isolation of topological entropy in non-Abelian quantum phases.

title: Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model in a quantum glassy landscape
summary: We study a generalization of ‘‘Yukawa models’’ in which Majorana fermions, interacting via all-to-all random couplings as in the Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev (SYK) model, are parametrically coupled to disordered bosonic degrees of freedom described by a quantum $p−\text{spin}$ model. The latter has its own non…

title: Nonadiabatic origin of quantum-metric effects via momentum-space metric tensor
summary: We reveal a fundamental geometric structure of momentum space arising from the nonadiabatic evolution of Bloch electrons. By extending semiclassical wave-packet theory to incorporate nonadiabatic effects, we introduce a momentum-space metric tensor—the nonadiabatic metric. This metric gives rise to …

title: Quantum Mpemba effect in long-ranged U(1)-symmetric random circuits
summary: The Mpemba effect, where a state prepared farther from equilibrium relaxes faster to equilibrium than one prepared closer, has a quantum counterpart where relaxation is resolved by conserved charge. However, the fate of the quantum Mpemba effect in systems with long-range interactions remains an ope…

title: Unified theoretical framework for spin dynamics excitation by the magnetic field of a high-frequency electromagnetic pulse
summary: Terahertz electromagnetic pulses offer a promising route for the ultrafast manipulation of magnetization in ferromagnetic materials. While previous studies have demonstrated the excitation of spin dynamics using linearly polarized terahertz fields, the role of circular polarization and the effects o…

title: Enhanced Andreev reflection in flat-band systems: Wave packet dynamics, dc transport, and the Josephson effect
summary: We investigate Andreev reflection (AR) in a proximity-induced normal-superconductor (NS) junction within the extended $α–{\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice, emphasizing the impact of flat bands on AR. Our findings reveal that flat bands significantly enhance AR. Through wave-packet dynamics, we track the re…

title: Anderson localization in spatially structured random graphs
summary: We study Anderson localization on high-dimensional graphs with spatial structure induced by long-ranged but distance-dependent hopping. To this end, we introduce a class of models that interpolate between the short-range Anderson model on a random regular graph and fully connected models with statis…

title: Quantum dynamics of monitored free fermions: Evolution of quantum correlations and scaling at measurement-induced phase transitions
summary: We explore, both analytically and numerically, the quantum dynamics of a many-body free-fermion system subjected to local density measurements. We begin by extending the mapping to the nonlinear sigma-model (NLSM) field theory for the case of finite evolution time $T$ and different classes of initia…

title: Krylov complexity under Hamiltonian deformations and Toda flows
summary: The quantum dynamics of a complex system can be efficiently described in Krylov space, the minimal subspace in which the dynamics unfolds. We apply the Krylov subspace method to Hamiltonian deformations, which provides a systematic way to construct solvable models from known instances. In doing so, …

title: Worldline deconfinement and emergent long-range interaction in the entanglement Hamiltonian and in the entanglement spectrum
summary: The entanglement spectrum (ES) is a powerful tool for probing topological phases. While its behavior in gapped systems is well understood, its properties in gapless regimes remain unclear. In this work, we employ a quantum Monte Carlo method to study the ES of a two-dimensional square-octagon lattic…

title: Gor'kov-Hedin-Baym equations for quantum many-body systems with spin-dependent interactions
summary: Driven by the need to understand and determine the presence of nontrivial superconductivity in real candidate materials, we present a generalized set of self-consistent Gor'kov-Hedin-Baym equations with spin-dependent electron-electron and electron-phonon interactions. This extends Hedin's original …

title: Odd-parity magnetism in Fe-based superconductors with coplanar magnetic order
summary: Odd-parity magnetism constitutes an intriguing phase of matter which breaks inversion symmetry while preserving time-reversal symmetry. Here we demonstrate that the Fe-based superconductors exhibiting coplanar magnetic order realize an odd-parity magnetic state by combining low-energy modeling with …

title: Interplay of magnetism and topological band robustness in layered rare-earth diantimonides
summary: Layered compounds $R\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{2}$ ($R$ = light rare earth), which first hosted the discovery of non-rivial topological fermions in nonmagnetic $\mathrm{La}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{2}$, offer an exceptional platform for probing the interplay between magnetism and topological electronic s…

title: Various electronic crystal phases in rhombohedral graphene multilayers
summary: We systematically investigate the emergence of electron crystal phases in rhombohedral multilayer graphene using comprehensive self-consistent Hartree Fock calculations combined with ab initio tight binding model. As the carrier density increases, we uncover an isospin cascade sequence of phase tran…

title: Exceptional thermoelectric properties in ${\mathrm{Na}}_{2}\mathrm{TlSb}$ enabled by quasi-one-dimensional band structure
summary: Materials with reduced dimensionality offer beneficial density-of-states (DOS) profiles for thermoelectric energy conversion, but can be impractical in realistic devices. Encouragingly, bulk high-symmetry materials can also exhibit similar quasi-low-dimensional band structures. A striking example is…

title: Controlled polarization switch in a polariton Josephson junction
summary: The interaction between a particle's spin and momentum—known as spin-orbit (SO) coupling—is the cornerstone of modern spintronics. In Bose-Einstein condensates of ultracold atoms, SO coupling can be implemented and precisely controlled experimentally; photonic systems, on the other hand, possess an …

title: Laser-induced commensurate-incommensurate transition of charge order in a Hubbard superlattice
summary: We investigate the nonequilibrium dynamics of charge density waves in a pumped one-dimensional Hubbard superlattice with staggered on-site Coulomb interactions at half filling, using time-dependent exact diagonalization. In equilibrium, the system exhibits commensurate charge correlations consistent…

title: Hund-projected Kanamori model: An effective description of Hund's metals near the Mott insulating regime
summary: Hund's coupling plays a decisive role in shaping electron correlations of multi-orbital systems, giving rise to a class of materials—Hund's metals—that combine local-moment physics with metallic transport. Here, we derive an effective low-energy description of such a system near the Mott insulating …

title: Isotopic enrichment of silicon: A molecular dynamics study on $^{28}\mathrm{Si}$ ion implantation at low energies
summary: High-fluence silicon-28 ($^{28}\mathrm{Si}$) ion implantation has been shown to produce isotopically enriched surface regions within natural silicon (Si) wafers through localized focused ion beam implantation. In this work, molecular dynamics simulations are performed to simulate $^{28}\mathrm{Si}$ …

title: Perfect spin-valley polarization and anomalous magnetoresistance in magnetic ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ superlattices
summary: In this work, the spin-valley dependent transmission properties in magnetic superlattices (MSLs) based on monolayer transition metal dichalcogenides, especially tungsten diselenide ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$, are studied. Ferromagnetic strips and top electrostatic gates are periodically distributed to gen…

title: Confinement-induced Majorana modes in a nodal topological superconductor
summary: We investigate the topological phase diagram of an extension of the Haldane model with equal spin pairing superconductivity. In two dimensions, we find a topological nodal superconducting phase, which exhibits a chiral Majorana mode propagating along the edges of nanoribbons with cylindrical boundar…
